subject: English Language
options: round the town twice before supper
round the town before supper twice
round twice the town before supper
the town found before supper twice
twice round town before the supper
explanation: This option places "twice" after "round the town," which is the correct word order for indicating the number of times the action took place.

subject: English Language
q: The Lagos Trade Fair________ annually.
explanation: This is because the Lagos Trade Fair takes place annually, which is a habitual or regular action, so the singular form "is held" is used.

subject: English Language
q: He promised to do the work in no time. This means he would do the work__________
explanation: The phrase "in no time" means that something will be done very quickly or in a very short period.

subject: English Language
q: The house is going under the hammer. This means the house is being___________
explanation: The phrase "going under the hammer" is an idiom commonly used to describe a property or item being auctioned. In an auction setting, the auctioneer often uses a hammer to signal the final bid and declare the item as sold to the highest bidder.

subject: English Language
q: The Senate President was impeached for being too compromising with the executive and will replace him with a more official.
explanation: The word "compromising" means being willing to make concessions or accommodate the other party. The option that is most nearly opposite in meaning to "compromising" is: rigid. "Rigid" means inflexible or unwilling to change, which contrasts with the idea of being compromising.

subject: English Language
q: Evelyn believed in a pragmatic approach, whereas Tina’s approach is ..........
explanation: The word "pragmatic" refers to a practical, realistic approach to problems or situations. The option that is most nearly opposite in meaning to "pragmatic" is theoretical. This is because "theoretical" refers to ideas or concepts that are abstract or not grounded in practical experience.

subject: English Language
q: John’s father has just bought a______________ car.
explanation:
This option follows the correct order of adjectives in English: opinion (brand new), size/shape/color (blue), origin (Japanese), and type (sports).

So, the sentence would read:
"John’s father has just bought a brand new blue Japanese sports car."

subject: English Language
q: Faruk _______ down while running the marathon.
explanation:
This is because the sentence suggests that Faruk was subject to an action (being pushed) while running the marathon, and this is best expressed in the passive form in the past continuous tense.

So, the sentence would read:
"Faruk was pushed down while running the marathon."

subject: English Language
q: Its high time we____________ discriminating against one another.
explanation:
The phrase "It's high time" is often followed by a verb in the past tense to suggest that something should have been done earlier, or it is now overdue. Therefore, "stopped" is the correct form to use here.

So, the sentence would read:
"It's high time we stopped discriminating against one another."

subject: English Language
q: The recent policy of the Central Bank about introducing higher denominations of the Naira has NOT allayed our fears about inflation; it has them as we had expected.
explanation: To "allay" means to put worries to rest, to ease. The opposite would be to "aggravate" which means to make a problem worse.

subject: English Language
q: The driver inadvertently killed a man in an auto crash and was consequently convicted of ....................
explanation: Manslaughter refers to the unlawful killing of a person without premeditation and due to negligence or an accident, which fits the context of the driver inadvertently killing a man.

subject: English Language
q: The Hausa language_______ in many parts of Africa.
explanation: The sentence refers to a general fact that is true in the present, so "is spoken" is the most appropriate choice.

subject: English Language
q: I have never seen such a_________ dog always in the mood to attack.
explanation: A "vicious" dog refers to one that is aggressive, violent or prone to attacking, which fits the description of a dog always in the mood to attack. A "voracious" animal is one that has a large appetite.

subject: English Language
q: Despite the Herculean task before the President, he still takes time off_________ to the Press.
explanation: The phrase "takes time off to talk" is grammatically correct. It implies that the President makes time for the purpose of talking to the press.

subject: English Language
q: The___________ whale suddenly attacked the vessel on the Atlantic Ocean.
explanation: The word "gigantic" means extremely large. It is the best description for a whale attacking a vessel in the ocean.

subject: English Language
q:
After the successful operation, he recovered by leaps and bound.
This means  ___________
options: His well-bound wounds aided his recovery in the middle of the night.
He did a lot of keep-fit exercises.
explanation: The expression "recovered by leaps and bounds" is an idiom that means someone's recovery or improvement is happening very rapidly and significantly. It suggests a swift and considerable progress in the person's health or well-being. Therefore, option A, "He got well very rapidly," is the most appropriate interpretation. The phrase "leaps and bounds" is commonly used to describe quick and substantial advancements or improvements in various contexts.

subject: English Language
q: He was instructed to catch the thief dead or ______.
explanation: The opposite of "dead" is "alive".

subject: English Language
q: The tax rebate was a concession given to the civil servants.
explanation: A "concession" in this context refers to something granted to relieve a burden, which is similar to "relief."

subject: English Language
q: Tolu___________ here before my arrival.
explanation: The sentence refers to an action that was completed before the arrival, which requires the past perfect tense, "had left."

subject: English Language
q: The boy is patently a rogue.
explanation: The word "patently" means something that is clearly or obviously true. Therefore, "obviously" is the nearest in meaning to "patently."

subject: English Language
q: I am not going to do______________.
explanation: The sentence would read: "I am not going to do any more work today." This is the correct word order for expressing that no additional work will be done for the remainder of the day.

subject: English Language
q: The man said that he________________
options: would sleep here a month ago
will sleep there a month ago
had slept there a month before
explanation: The sentence would read: "The man said that he had slept there a month before." This is the correct use of the past perfect tense, which is used to describe an action completed before another action in the past.

subject: English Language
q: It may be unwise to consider any________ man your enemy.
explanation: The sentence would read: "It may be unwise to consider any mortal man your enemy." In this context, "mortal" refers to any human being.

subject: English Language
q: The jilted woman eventually found herself at_______.
explanation: The phrase "at a crossroads" is an idiomatic expression to indicate a point of decision or an important juncture in life. Therefore, the sentence would read: "The jilted woman eventually found herself at a crossroads."

subject: English Language
q: The new refinery will turn______ five million barrels of oil daily.
explanation:
The phrase "turn out" means to produce or manufacture something. For example, if a factory is said to "turn out" a certain number of products per day, it means the factory produces that many products daily.

So, in the sentence "The new refinery will turn out five million barrels of oil daily," the word "out" is used to indicate the production or processing of five million barrels of oil on a daily basis by the new refinery.

subject: English Language
q: He denied that he________ the man.
explanation: In reported speech, "denied that he had seen" is appropriate because it indicates a past event that the speaker is denying.

subject: English Language
q: Wole Soyinka gave his _________country a great award.
explanation: In this context, "home country" best fits the sentence, as it refers to Wole Soyinka's native or birth country.

subject: English Language
q: The impertinent woman has just left here with her bag and baggage. This means she left with all her________.
explanation: The phrase "with her bag and baggage" means that the woman left with all of her belongings or possessions.

subject: English Language
q: In computing, data is processed to give ___________
explanation: The word "information" is an uncountable noun, so it does not take an article like "an" or a plural form like "informations."

subject: English Language
q: No sensible government will welcome subversive criticisms from the public.
explanation: "Subversive" describes actions or ideas intended to undermine or overthrow an established system in a harmful or destructive way. Therefore, "destructive" is the nearest in meaning to "subversive" in this context.

subject: English Language
q: To keep our bodies healthy, we need a_________ diet.
explanation: A "balanced diet" refers to a diet that includes a variety of nutrients in the right proportions, which is essential for keeping our bodies healthy.

subject: English Language
q: If he_______ there, he would have met him at home
explanation: The sentence requires the past perfect tense, "had gone," because it refers to a hypothetical situation in the past. The correct structure for third conditional sentences is "If + past perfect, ... would have + past participle."

subject: English Language
q: Some people believe that it is ominous for the dog to bark at night.
explanation: "Ominous" refers to something that suggests or indicates a future event, often a negative or threatening one. Therefore, "threatening" is the nearest in meaning to "ominous."

subject: English Language
q: Many people watched the kidnapper_________ by the angry mob.
explanation: The sentence describes an ongoing action (the kidnapper is in the process of being lynched by the mob). Therefore, "being lynched" is the most appropriate choice.

subject: English Language
q: The introduction of religious fanaticism in state affairs is an ill wind that blows no one any good. This means that if is a ..........development.
explanation: The phrase "an ill wind that blows no one any good" refers to a harmful or negative development. Therefore, introducing religious fanaticism into state affairs would be considered a dangerous development.

subject: English Language
q: If I_____ you, l would forgive the boy.
explanation: The sentence uses the conditional "If I were you," which is a common way to express a hypothetical situation in English. This structure is known as the subjunctive mood.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options A to E the one that MOST APPROPRIATELY COMPLETES THE SENTENCE

The Chairman with all his Councilors______ honoured last weekend
explanation: When any of these words: with, along with, together with, as well as, in addition to, including, no less than, etc. joins a compound subject, the form of the verb is singular. Therefore 'was' should be the verb that accompanies the compound subject: "The chairman with all his councillors".

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options A to E the one that is MOST NEARLY OPPOSITE IN MEANING to the underlined word.

Religious people are expected to be ascetic.
explanation: In the context of religious practices, asceticism is about self-discipline, modesty, and often renouncing material comforts for spiritual purposes. Therefore, "flamboyant" is a better opposite as it conveys a vibrant, extravagant style that goes against the understated and disciplined nature of asceticism.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options labelled A to E, the one that contains the sound represented by the given symbol.

| b|

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options labeled A to E, the most appropriate question to the given sentence containing an emphatic stress word. The emphatic stress word is written in CAPITAL letters.


Farmers PLANT crops during the rainy season.
options: Do farmers harvest crops during the rainy season?
Do farmers level crops during the rainy season?
Do farmers plant crops before the rainy season?
Do farmers plant crops during the dry season?
Do labourers plant crops during the rainy season?
explanation: The rule is to select a question that contrasts with the emphasized word/ or the word in capital letters. In this case, the word 'plant' contrasts with 'harvest'. Therefore the answer is A. Do the farmers harvest crops during the rainy season?

explanation: Say what you will" is a common idiom that expresses a disregard for someone's opinions or objections. It implies that the speaker is determined to proceed regardless of what others say.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options A to E the one that is the NEAREST IN MEANING TO THE UNDERLINED WORD.

The man who purportedly stole the missing goats disappeared miraculously.
explanation: The underlined word "purportedly" means claimed or said to be true, but not necessarily proven. The closest synonym among the options is "allegedly", which also means that something is stated as a fact without conclusive proof.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options A to E the one that MOST APPROPRIATELY COMPLETES THE SENTENCE

Although the boy was given ........ he went ahead and did as he pleased.
explanation: "Advice" is an uncountable noun, so it does not take a plural form.  The correct phrase is "valuable advice", which does not require an article because advice is uncountable and general.

Chief Garba is the live wire of our club. This means Chief Garba is
explanation: The expression "live wire" is an idiom. When someone is referred to as "the live wire", the person is very active and has a lot of energy.

No sooner had the President entered the hall ............the programme started.
explanation:
The phrase "No sooner... than" is a standard correlative conjunction used to indicate that one action happened immediately after another.

Thus, the correct sentence is: "No sooner had the President entered the hall than the programme started."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options labelled A to E, the most appropriate question to the given sentence containing; an emphatic stress word. The emphatic stress word is written in CAPITAL letters. ..........


Mary STOLE the biro in Tunde’s locker.
options: Did Mary borrow the biro in Tunde‘s locker?
Did Mary return the biro to Tunde‘s locker?
Did Mary steal the biro in Ayo’s locker?
Did Mary steal the biro in Tunde‘s bag?
Did Rosaline steal the book in Tunde’s locker?
explanation:
When answering questions on emphatic stress, the option that negates or contradicts the word on emphasis is the correct option

From the above question, the emphatic stress is placed on the word ''stole''. So we look out for options that doesn't contain ''stole'' and completely negates the word. 

Option A is correct because the word ''borrow'' contadicts the emphasized word ''stole''.

mary stole the biro in Tunde's locker. mary did not borrow the biro.

explanation:
The word "industrious" describes someone who is hardworking, diligent, and actively engaged in their work. The opposite of "industrious" would be someone who is lazy or not engaged in productive activities.

Option E, "idle," is the most nearly opposite in meaning to "industrious." "Idle" refers to being inactive, lazy, or not engaged in work or activities. Therefore, in the context of the sentence, "idle" is the most appropriate choice to convey the opposite meaning to "industrious."

In this case, "each" is a singular pronoun, and when referring to a singular antecedent, the corresponding possessive pronoun should also be singular. Therefore, the correct completion is "Each of the authors received his royalties," where "his" agrees in number with the singular antecedent "each."

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options lettered A - E the one that most appropriately completes each sentence

It’s important for you and ..............to be there.
explanation:
In English grammar, pronouns like "me," "you," "him," "her," "it," "us," and "them" are used to replace nouns in sentences. In the sentence "It’s important for you and me to be there," the pronoun "me" is used as the object of the preposition "for." 

When using pronouns in compound subjects or objects (in this case, "you and me"), it's essential to determine whether the pronoun should be in the subjective (nominative) case or the objective case based on its function in the sentence.

- Subjective case pronouns are used as the subject of a sentence or clause. For example: "He and I went to the store."
- Objective case pronouns are used as the object of a verb or preposition. For example: "She gave the book to him and me."

In the sentence "It’s important for you and me to be there," "you" is the subject, and "me" is the object of the preposition "for," so "me" is the correct form to use.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options lettered A - E the one that most appropriately completes each sentence

Most rich men prefer travelling by air…………travelling by road.
explanation:
Most rich men prefer travelling by air to travelling by road. The correct grammatical structure for expressing preference is: prefer [A] to [B]. In this sentence: "travelling by air" is option A while "travelling by road" is option B

So we say: prefer travelling by air to travelling by road

"Cattle" is a plural noun that refers to cows or bulls collectively. It has no singular form like "cattles" — the word "cattles" is grammatically incorrect. "Head" of cattle is a traditional unit of counting livestock, especially cattle.
For example: He owns fifty head of cattle or They bought ten head of sheep.

subject: English Language
q: Choose from the options lettered A - E the one that has a different stress pattern from the others:
explanation:
The stress pattern in English typically involves the emphasis placed on certain syllables within a word. In this case, "impartial" has a different stress pattern compared to the other words:

  • Favour (A): Stress on the first syllable ("FA-vour").
  • Country (B): Stress on the first syllable ("COUN-try").
  • Mother (C): Stress on the first syllable ("MOTH-er").
  • Impartial (D): Stress on the second syllable ("im-PAR-tial").

The word "impartial" has the stress on the second syllable, making it different from the others, which have stress on the first syllable.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options lettered A - E the one that most appropriately completes each sentence

Paul together with his younger brother______discharged and acquitted by the magistrate last week.
explanation: The phrase "together with his younger brother" is an additional element in the sentence and does not affect the subject-verb agreement. The subject of the sentence is "Paul," and "together with his younger brother" is what is referred to as a false subject. Since "Paul" is a singular noun, the appropriate verb to use is the singular form "was."

subject: English Language
q:
Complete this statement with the most appropriate of the options lettered A- E

Though Chike has been ........ his colleagues believe that he committed the crime.
explanation: The word "Though" indicates a contrast, so we are looking for a word that shows Chike was officially cleared of guilt, but others still suspect him. To be exonerated means to show that someone or something is not guilty of something.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options lettered A- E the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the word or expression in italics

Your action is in negation of our earlier decision.
explanation: The word "negation" means denial, contradiction, or refusal. The opposite of this would be agreement or harmony with something. To be in consonance with means to agree with or be in harmony with, which is the direct opposite of negation. To be in connection with does not directly oppose "negation" as one can be connected to something without agreeing with it.

subject: English Language
q:
Complete this statement with the most appropriate of the options lettered A- E

After three consecutive months without pay at work, we are beginning to feel the____________
explanation: The phrase "feel the crunch" is an idiomatic expression that means experiencing pressure, difficulty or hardship, especially in financial or resource-related situations. In this sentence, "crunch" means the growing difficulty or hardship caused by the lack of income.

subject: English Language
q:
Choose from the options lettered A - E the one that most appropriately completes the sentence

I had __________ little time that I couldn’t the work.
explanation: "so" emphasises the extent or degree of the limited time. It indicates that the amount of time available was to such an extent that it was insufficient to complete the work.  So, the completed sentence reads: "I had so little time that I couldn't do the work."
